raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":0.7,"RX.4":0.3,"RX.5":0.3},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"To better understand your request to 'improve this draft', please choose the primary focus: (1) Enhancing writing style, grammar, and flow; (2) Improving logical consistency, evidence alignment, and structure; (3) Assessing novelty, citations, and conceptual framing; or (4) A comprehensive review covering all aspects.","rationale":{"R1.2":"The delegation to improve the draft centrally involves verifying the logical consistency of the argument chain to ensure premises lead coherently to conclusions.","R1.3":"Improving the draft requires checking evidence-claim alignment to confirm that reported results support the stated claims.","R1.5":"Structural reorganization is a central aspect of improving the draft to enhance section-level ordering and narrative arc.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing for improving the draft, focusing on sentence- and paragraph-level grammar, flow, and conciseness.","RX.1":"Uncertainty disclosure is centrally expected in improving a research draft to appropriately highlight assumptions and limitations like potential confounding.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is central to ensure claims about the ATE and effects are not exaggerated beyond the evidence and sensitivity analysis.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is central to keep improvements within the bounds of the deployment data and targeted estimand without overgeneralizing.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is peripheral but considered to prevent any unintended promotion of harmful applications in conversational systems.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability are peripheral, ensuring data sources and methods like AIPW estimation are clearly attributable."}}

parsed.clarification_question: To better understand your request to 'improve this draft', please choose the primary focus: (1) Enhancing writing style, grammar, and flow; (2) Improving logical consistency, evidence alignment, and structure; (3) Assessing novelty, citations, and conceptual framing; or (4) A comprehensive review covering all aspects.

rationale.R1.2: The delegation to improve the draft centrally involves verifying the logical consistency of the argument chain to ensure premises lead coherently to conclusions.
rationale.R1.3: Improving the draft requires checking evidence-claim alignment to confirm that reported results support the stated claims.
rationale.R1.5: Structural reorganization is a central aspect of improving the draft to enhance section-level ordering and narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing for improving the draft, focusing on sentence- and paragraph-level grammar, flow, and conciseness.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is centrally expected in improving a research draft to appropriately highlight assumptions and limitations like potential confounding.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is central to ensure claims about the ATE and effects are not exaggerated beyond the evidence and sensitivity analysis.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is central to keep improvements within the bounds of the deployment data and targeted estimand without overgeneralizing.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is peripheral but considered to prevent any unintended promotion of harmful applications in conversational systems.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are peripheral, ensuring data sources and methods like AIPW estimation are clearly attributable.
